Fault Code: C10E2 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Flashing LED on Parking Brake Switch • Parking Brake warning message in instrument cluster • Parking Brake fails to operate --- Possible Causes --- • Faulty Wiring or Connections • Faulty Automatic parking brake Motor • Low battery voltage (may cause component damage) --- Possible Solutions --- • Check/repair electrical wiring or connections • Replace faulty Automatic parking brake Motor --- Special Notes --- • When found in the 03-ABS Brake module on MQB (Mk7 chassis) vehicles with an Electro-Mechanical Parking Brake (EPB) seepost #8 in this thread

Fault Code: C123E --- Possible Symptoms --- • ABS warning lamp ON • ESC (ESP/ASR) warning lamp ON • Brake system warning lamp ON --- Possible Causes --- • Malfunction in the vacuum system for brake assistance • Vacuum leaks (lines, check valve, brake booster) • Defective or insufficient vacuum pump • Faulty brake booster or vacuum sensor • Hydraulic Boost Compensation (HBC) activated repeatedly due to loss of vacuum supply • Prolonged driving with unresolved vacuum system malfunction --- Special Notes --- • If there is a malfunction in the vacuum system for the braking assistance, the ABS control unit activates Hydraulic Boost Compensation (HBC) to maintain braking assistance. • However, only a limited number of HBC activations are permitted. Once this limit is reached, the ABS control unit permanently deactivates braking electronics and logs the event:C123EF0 Hydraulic brake servo limit reached. • At that point, the ABS unit must be replaced. • This is not a random failure of the ABS unit, but rather a safeguard triggered because a vacuum-related malfunction was not repaired in a timely manner. • See theBentley Motorcars Technical Service Bulletin 2053923– Although this bulletin is written for Bentley vehicles, the information and condition described may also apply to other Volkswagen Group brands

Fault Code: P0030 Standard OBD-II Code: P0030 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) ON --- Possible Causes --- • Faulty O2 sensor heater circuit • Wiring/Connector issue • Faulty ECM --- Possible Solutions --- • Inspect and test O2 sensor heater circuit as per repair manual • Inspect and test wiring harness as per wiring diagram • Replace ECM if O2 sensor and wiring harness are to factory design

Fault Code: P0088 Standard OBD-II Code: P0088 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) ON • High pitched whining noise from engine when idling. --- Possible Causes --- • Fault with the G247-Rail Fuel Pressure Sensor, wiring, connections, etc. • Fault with the N276-Fuel Pressure Regulator Valve, power, ground, connections, etc. • Fault with the N290- Fuel Metering Valve(If applicable) • Engine timing chain worn/stretched.(Only some 2006 to 2009 3.6L gasoline engines- See Special Notes) --- Possible Solutions --- • Check the Advanced Measuring Values for Rail pressures • Check power supply at the N276-Fuel Pressure Regulator Valve/N290- Fuel Metering Valve • Check the G247-Rail Fuel Pressure Sensor --- Special Notes --- • NAR (North American Region) VW Tech Tips Fault Code: P00AF/000175 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) ON • Glow Plug light flashing • Reduced engine performance --- Possible Causes --- • Defective N75 • N75 connector plugged into the wrong 2 wire component. Check wiring diagram to confirm • Vacuum leak or low vacuum • VNT sticking or seized • G581 sensor --- Possible Solutions --- • Verify N75 is working to design, use Output Testing • Verify the N75 connector plugged into N75 per the factory level wiring diagram rather than any other component that may not set a fault code such as N79 (Positive Crankcase Ventilation (PCV) heating element) • Verify vacuum lines are good, check engine vacuum at idle • Test VNT and G581, apply vacuum to VNT and watch MVB 120.4Engine off, no vacuum 120.4 should read about 3.5VEngine Off, apply 17 Inches of mercury, 120.4 should drop to about .7VEngine at idle, 120.4 should read about .7VIf 120.4 voltage doesn't alter, VNT may be seized or G581 failed • Engine off, no vacuum 120.4 should read about 3.5V • Engine Off, apply 17 Inches of mercury, 120.4 should drop to about .7V • Engine at idle, 120.4 should read about .7V • If 120.4 voltage doesn't alter, VNT may be seized or G581 failed • Engine off, no vacuum 120.4 should read about 3.5V • Engine Off, apply 17 Inches of mercury, 120.4 should drop to about .7V • Engine at idle, 120.4 should read about .7V • If 120.4 voltage doesn't alter, VNT may be seized or G581 failed --- Special Notes --- TDI • On someTDIengines, the Vacuum Diaphragm/G581 unit can be purchased as a separate part from dealer. • 2.0L CR TDIowners may find the following threads Fault Code: P00B7/000183 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Engine coolant temperature is outside controlled range • Power Loss • Reduced vehicle speed --- Possible Causes --- • Rotary Coolant Valve (RCV) malfunction • Faulty N493 - Engine Temperature Control ActuatorFaulty wiring/connectors • Faulty wiring/connectors • Faulty water pumpCooling system compromised • Cooling system compromised • Faulty wiring/connectors • Cooling system compromised --- Possible Solutions --- • Check/replace as needed N493 - Engine Temperature Control ActuatorCheck/repair wiring, connections for the N493 • Check/repair wiring, connections for the N493 • Check/replace water pump • Check coolant system for leaks • Check/repair wiring, connections for the N493 --- Special Notes --- • Many parts used to address cooling system fault codes have been superseded. We recommend using the latest original manufacturer parts. Please consult your local parts supplier for details.

Fault Code: P0263 Standard OBD-II Code: P0263 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) ON --- Possible Causes --- • Engine controller software out of date.See special notes • Replacement Fuel Injectors (Injector Quantity Adjustment (IMA) channels) not calibrated to engine controller • Faulty Fuel Injector • Low compression or engine mechanical issues • Carbon deposits on Pressure Sensor/Glow Plugs including Cylinder specific Internal Pressure Sensor DTCs --- Possible Solutions --- • Check/Update Engine controller software.See special notes • Check Injector Quantity Adjustment (IMA) Adaptation channels • Check/Replace Faulty Fuel Injector and calibrate IMA Adaptation channels • Check the Engine mechanical condition in addition to the installed Pressure Sensor/Glow Plugs --- Special Notes --- • CR TDI engine controllers had several known software issues and dealer level flash updates over the years. Fault Code: P0336 Standard OBD-II Code: P0336 --- Special Notes --- • When found in the 2005-2008 (4F) Chassis Audi A6 3.2L BKH engine controller:Make sure the G28 wires from the sensor to the Engine controller are correctly wired per the wiring diagram.Reverse polarity (swapped wires) will cause this DTC in that particular car.The G28 testing procedure in the factory level repair manual is either incorrect -or- not applicable to ALL 3.2L BKH engine controllers.The lack of 12V (Battery voltage) does not indicate a faulty module. Live testing revealed roughly 2.5V on each wire in a fully functional 2006 3.2L A6. • Make sure the G28 wires from the sensor to the Engine controller are correctly wired per the wiring diagram.Reverse polarity (swapped wires) will cause this DTC in that particular car. • Reverse polarity (swapped wires) will cause this DTC in that particular car. • The G28 testing procedure in the factory level repair manual is either incorrect -or- not applicable to ALL 3.2L BKH engine controllers.The lack of 12V (Battery voltage) does not indicate a faulty module. Fault Code: P047F/00115 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) ON --- Possible Causes --- • Fuse(s) faulty • Wiring and/or Connector(s) from/to Exhaust Pressure Flap Control Module (J883) faulty • Exhaust Pressure Flap Control Module (J883) faulty --- Possible Solutions --- • Check Fuse(s) • Check Wiring and/or Connector(s) from/to Exhaust Pressure Flap Control Module (J883) • Check/Replace Exhaust Pressure Flap Control Module (J883)Perform Output Test/Basic Setting/Adaptation when applicable • Perform Output Test/Basic Setting/Adaptation when applicable • Perform Output Test/Basic Setting/Adaptation when applicable --- Special Notes --- • When found inVW Golf/Jetta (1K): 2.0l CR-TDI (CBEA/CJAA):This Fault Code may show up in combination or shortly after flashing the Engine Control Module.Verify the mechanical part of the Exhaust Valve Control Module (J883) is not seized or binding. Water in the exhaust system causes the flap motor to rust at the pivot points and these replacement modules have been revised several times in the parts catalog. See TSB # 26-12-08 -or- TPI# 2031583 for NAR market vehicles.Most of these NAR CR-TDI engines support Basic Settings of the J883 component and this is advised after replacement. When connected to the vehicle with VCDS select the applicable basic settings from the menu with the vehicle at idle, click GO and On/Off/Next. The self test may take up to two minutes to complete and the baseline information inthis threadmay be helpful. Fault Code: P0491 Standard OBD-II Code: P0491 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) active --- Possible Causes --- • Fuse or relay for Secondary Air Injection Pump faulty • Hose(s)/Piping between Secondary Air Injection Pump and Combi (Secondary Air Injection) Valve leaking • Secondary Air Injection Solenoid(s) faulty • Secondary Air Injection Pressure Sensor faulty • Combi Valve(s) faulty • Secondary Air Injection ports in the cylinder head(s) plugged with carbonSee Special Notes --- Possible Solutions --- • Check fuse and relay for Secondary Air Injection Pump Fault Code: P052F --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) active • Glow Plug Indicator Light active --- Possible Causes --- • Voltage error received from the J179-Automatic Glow Time Control ModuleVoltage supply 0.0V (via LIN-Bus message) • Voltage supply 0.0V (via LIN-Bus message) • Voltage supply 0.0V (via LIN-Bus message) --- Possible Solutions --- • Check power/ground for J179-Automatic Glow Time Control Module

Fault Code: P053F/001343 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) ON --- Possible Causes --- • Fault code will typically set when the difference between Actual and Specified Rail pressure is 15-Bar or 1.50-MPa • Poor fuel quantity delivery(In tank pump, fuel filter, damaged fuel lines, etc). • Fault with the G247-Rail Fuel Pressure Sensor, wiring, connections, etc. • Fault with the N276-Fuel Pressure Regulator Valve, power, ground, connections, etc. --- Possible Solutions --- • Check the in-tank fuel pressure with a mechanical gauge during startup. • Check the Advanced Measuring Values for Rail pressures • Check power supply at the N276-Fuel Pressure Regulator Valve • Check the G247-Rail Fuel Pressure Sensor --- Special Notes --- • ECM may also have stored in memory,P0088- Fuel Rail/System Pressure: Too High. • With the 1.8L/2.0L engines, if fault codeP0016- Bank 1: Camshaft Position Sensor (G40)/Engine Speed Sensor (G28): Incorrect Correlationis also stored, please use this Ross-Tech forum page to verify engine timing is correct.Audi Volkswagen - 2.0L Chain Drive - P0016 Cam/Crank correlation check • Audi Volkswagen - 2.0L Chain Drive - P0016 Cam/Crank correlation check • Audi Volkswagen - 2.0L Chain Drive - P0016 Cam/Crank correlation check • When found in 2006 to 2021 3.6L Audi/Volkswagen engines with engine codes [BLV, BHK, CDVB, CNNA, CGRA, CDVC,] please see the Volkswagen Technical Tip [01-20-50](2060799)https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2022/MC-10222093-0001.pdfFuel Rail pressure is excessively highCheck Camshaft adaptations, must not exceed [-8.0 to +8.0]Non-UDS (2006~2011) vehicles use Measuring Value Block group 208 and 209, field 3UDS (2011 and newer) vehicles use Advanced Measuring Values, IDE00182 and IDE00184Fault codes P0087, P0088 and/or P1025 may be present due to the High Pressure Fuel Pump being out of time. • Fuel Rail pressure is excessively high • Check Camshaft adaptations, must not exceed [-8.0 to +8.0]Non-UDS (2006~2011) vehicles use Measuring Value Block group 208 and 209, field 3UDS (2011 and newer) vehicles use Advanced Measuring Values, IDE00182 and IDE00184 • Non-UDS (2006~2011) vehicles use Measuring Value Block group 208 and 209, field 3 • UDS (2011 and newer) vehicles use Advanced Measuring Values, IDE00182 and IDE00184 • Fault codes P0087, P0088 and/or P1025 may be present due to the High Pressure Fuel Pump being out of time. • Fuel Rail pressure is excessively high • Check Camshaft adaptations, must not exceed [-8.0 to +8.0]Non-UDS (2006~2011) vehicles use Measuring Value Block group 208 and 209, field 3UDS (2011 and newer) vehicles use Advanced Measuring Values, IDE00182 and IDE00184 • Non-UDS (2006~2011) vehicles use Measuring Value Block group 208 and 209, field 3 • UDS (2011 and newer) vehicles use Advanced Measuring Values, IDE00182 and IDE00184 • Fault codes P0087, P0088 and/or P1025 may be present due to the High Pressure Fuel Pump being out of time. • Non-UDS (2006~2011) vehicles use Measuring Value Block group 208 and 209, field 3 • UDS (2011 and newer) vehicles use Advanced Measuring Values, IDE00182 and IDE00184

Fault Code: P0556 Standard OBD-II Code: P0556 --- Special Notes --- • When found in:The NAR (North American market) market CZTA 1.4T Jetta:Make sure the 01-Engine control module is up to date per TPI 2045383 (TSB 01-18-09) on M.Y. 2016 - 2017 vehicles.https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2016/MC-10130153-9999.pdfNHTSAMake sure the (V192) Brake system vacuum pump, (J57) Vacuum pump relay and fuse are OK. The 1.4L gasoline engine CZTA(AW0) from July 2015uses fuses SB4 and SB16 but verify the car in question using theOfficial Factory Repair Information. Because Selective Output Tests and Basic Settings for those components are NOT supported by the ECU it will be necessary to diagnose those components manually.The NAR market 2013 - 2016 Jetta Hybrid:See Tech Tip 01-16-04. That document describes manually testing the vacuum pump and relay but also makes note that the relay may be in positions R5 -or- R6. Fault Code: P068A/001674 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) ON --- Possible Causes --- • Fuse(s) faulty • Ignition Switch faulty • Wiring and/or Connector(s) from/to Power Supply Relay (J271/J317) faulty • Power Supply Relay (J271/J317) faulty --- Possible Solutions --- • Check Fuse(s) • Check Wiring and/or Connector(s) from/to Power Supply Relay (J271/J317) • Check/Replace Power Supply Relay (J271/J317) --- Special Notes --- • A weak Battery may set this fault code. Insure the Battery voltage is at least 11.5v. • 2009-2014 Audi vehicles with no start issues, please see TSB 97-14-2 or TPI 2036764 for possible updated J271 relay.https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2014/SB-10055713-5611.pdfNHTSA • https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2014/SB-10055713-5611.pdfNHTSA • https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2014/SB-10055713-5611.pdfNHTSA

Fault Code: P06A3/001699 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) active • Poor engine performance --- Possible Causes --- • Low ECM voltage to ECM • Faulty wiring on Sensor Reference Voltage "D" Circuit • Faulty sensor or component on Sensor Reference Voltage "D" Circuit • Faulty ECM --- Possible Solutions --- • Verify ALL Powers and Grounds for the ECM • Check wiring on Sensor Reference Voltage "D" Circuit • Check sensors and components on the Sensor Reference Voltage "D" Circuit • Check wiring at the ECM with all components removed from the Circuit to verify the reference voltage is present. --- Special Notes --- • See this Ross-Tech forum post, when found in vehicles with the CBEA/CJAA 2.0l CR-TDI engineshttps://forums.ross-tech.com/index.php?threads/33187/#post-280391 • https://forums.ross-tech.com/index.php?threads/33187/#post-280391 • https://forums.ross-tech.com/index.php?threads/33187/#post-280391

(J1120) see NAR market TPI 2051553.NHTSAhttps://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2018/MC-10145085-9999.pdfThe capacitor system can be identified in the VCDS can based onComponent: BMS_48V_SCAPAdditional DTCs covered by that TPI include:P0A9E,P0AC8andP0ECAwith various 48V chassis control issues in Address D4-Roll Control 1 and Address D5-Roll Control 2. • NHTSAhttps://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2018/MC-10145085-9999.pdf • The capacitor system can be identified in the VCDS can based onComponent: BMS_48V_SCAP • Additional DTCs covered by that TPI include:P0A9E,P0AC8andP0ECAwith various 48V chassis control issues in Address D4-Roll Control 1 and Address D5-Roll Control 2. • NHTSAhttps://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2018/MC-10145085-9999.pdf • The capacitor system can be identified in the VCDS can based onComponent: BMS_48V_SCAP • Additional DTCs covered by that TPI include:P0A9E,P0AC8andP0ECAwith various 48V chassis control issues in Address D4-Roll Control 1 and Address D5-Roll Control 2.

Fault Code: P1065 Standard OBD-II Code: P1065 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) active • Engine performance issues --- Possible Causes --- • Fuel Pressure too High or Low • Mechanical Deficiency of Hardware (worn or missing parts) • Missing Basic Settings or Adaptation after installing replacement parts. --- Possible Solutions --- • When found in a CR TDI see common issues related to16471/P0087/000135 • The following Basic Settings resolved this DTC in a 2011 CAHA 2.0 TDI after replacement of N290 and all damaged fuel system parts:[*IDE02703*] Reset learned values quant. meter. unit and press. contr. valveThe EDC required the Security Access code shown in the pop-up balloon before the Basic Setting group was available.That Basic Settings was performed Key On, Engine Off.E?ID=439317 • [*IDE02703*] Reset learned values quant. meter. unit and press. contr. valve • The EDC required the Security Access code shown in the pop-up balloon before the Basic Setting group was available. • That Basic Settings was performed Key On, Engine Off.E?ID=439317 • [*IDE02703*] Reset learned values quant. meter. unit and press. contr. valve • The EDC required the Security Access code shown in the pop-up balloon before the Basic Setting group was available. • That Basic Settings was performed Key On, Engine Off.E?ID=439317

Fault Code: P106B/004203 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Lamp (MIL) active --- Possible Causes --- • Wiring/Connectors from/to Throttle Body Control Module to Engine Control Module faulty • Throttle Body Control Module Damaged and/or Moving Difficulties --- Possible Solutions --- • Check Wiring/Connectors from/to Throttle Body Control Module to Engine Control ModuleCheck for properly attached Connectors, twisted Pins, loose Contact, Corrosion and Water Ingress • Check for properly attached Connectors, twisted Pins, loose Contact, Corrosion and Water Ingress • Check Throttle Body Control Module for Damage and Moving Difficulties • Perform (Selective) Output Test • Check for properly attached Connectors, twisted Pins, loose Contact, Corrosion and Water Ingress --- Special Notes --- • When found on 3.0l CR-TDI (i.e. BKS/BUG) or 4.2l CR-TDI (BVN), the Learning Values have to be cleared after replacing the Throttle Body Control Module.DO NOT CLEAR LEARNED VALUES IF YOU HAVE NOT REPLACED THE THROTTLE BODY CONTROL MODULE!Otherwise the proper values are being cleared and incorrect values are being learned. • DO NOT CLEAR LEARNED VALUES IF YOU HAVE NOT REPLACED THE THROTTLE BODY CONTROL MODULE!Otherwise the proper values are being cleared and incorrect values are being learned. • DO NOT CLEAR LEARNED VALUES IF YOU HAVE NOT REPLACED THE THROTTLE BODY CONTROL MODULE!Otherwise the proper values are being cleared and incorrect values are being learned. [Select][01 - Engine](for Bank 1/J388) or[11 - Engine 2](for Bank 2/J544) [Coding-II - 11] • Enable the Learning Procedure by Entering10021. [Do It!][Done, Go Back][Close Controller, Go Back - 06] • Switch the Ignition OFF. • Switch the Ignition ON for 1 Second, then turn it OFF immediately. • Wait at least 30 Seconds, then switch the Ignition ON again. • Now Check if all remaining fault codes can be cleared.

Fault Code: P10A4 --- Special Notes --- When found in the Audi A6 (4F) and Audi Q7 (4L) models with 3.0L supercharged gas engines such as CCAA, CJWE, CJWC and CJWB this DTC points to a problem with Supercharger Control Valve (J808). It is NOT an IMRC system DTC. : • Check wiring to/from J808. • Verify J808 is not oil soaked or dirty. • See Basic Settings and Measuring Block group 168. • For UDS ECM, Basic Settings IDE02226 - Adaptation of regulating flap for compressor Internal ref to ST?ID=86822

Fault Code: P1138 Standard OBD-II Code: P1138 --- Possible Solutions --- • Check Mass Air Flow (MAF) Sensor • Check Fuel Pressure Regulator • Check Fuel Pump • Check Intake/Exhaust System for Leaks • Check Secondary Air Injection for Leaks • Check Vacuum Lines for Leaks --- Special Notes --- • Fuel Trim Info • When this fault is stored and external vacuum leaks are not found, less obvious problems may be present:Check Evaporative Emission (EVAP) Canister Purge Regulator Valve (N80)Check Brake Booster and HosesCheck Electric Vacuum Pump ( If Applicable )Check Crankcase Ventilation System for faulty components. When the PCV or Crankcase Ventilation System is faulty, vacuum is typically present under the oil cap and misfire faults are common. • Check Evaporative Emission (EVAP) Canister Purge Regulator Valve (N80) • Check Brake Booster and Hoses • Check Electric Vacuum Pump ( If Applicable ) • Check Crankcase Ventilation System for faulty components. When the PCV or Crankcase Ventilation System is faulty, vacuum is typically present under the oil cap and misfire faults are common. • Check Evaporative Emission (EVAP) Canister Purge Regulator Valve (N80) • Check Brake Booster and Hoses • Check Electric Vacuum Pump ( If Applicable ) • Check Crankcase Ventilation System for faulty components. When the PCV or Crankcase Ventilation System is faulty, vacuum is typically present under the oil cap and misfire faults are common.

Fault Code: P119A/004506 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) active • Reduced fuel economy and engine performance --- Possible Causes --- • Faulty Fuel Pressure Sensor (G247) • Wiring and/or Connector(s) from/to Fuel Pressure Sensor (G247) • Faulty HPFP • Faulty (leaking) fuel injector • Exhaust Leak (sounds strange, but check for one!) --- Possible Solutions --- • Replace Fuel Pressure Sensor (G247) • Check Wiring and/or Connector(s) from/to Fuel Pressure Sensor (G247) • Replace HPFP • Check fuel injectors for leak-down • Fix exhaust leak if one is found. --- Special Notes --- • The Fuel Pressure Regulator Valve (N276) or Fuel Metering valve (N290) on the HPFP can fail.(N276)Fuel Pressure Regulating Valveresistance should be approximately 1 to 5 Ω *(N290)Fuel Metering valveresistance should be approximately 0.8 to 5 Ω *(* always refer to repair manuals for specific values for your engine) • (N276)Fuel Pressure Regulating Valveresistance should be approximately 1 to 5 Ω * • (N290)Fuel Metering valveresistance should be approximately 0.8 to 5 Ω * • (* always refer to repair manuals for specific values for your engine) • (N276)Fuel Pressure Regulating Valveresistance should be approximately 1 to 5 Ω * • (N290)Fuel Metering valveresistance should be approximately 0.8 to 5 Ω * • (* always refer to repair manuals for specific values for your engine) • If found with a TFSI engine and16555/P0171/000369- Fuel Trim; Bank 1: System Too Lean is also flagged, this could be caused by a failed rear main seal.TFSI - Oil leak - TFSI - Oil leak - Not a VCDS related question? or is it? • TFSI - Oil leak - TFSI - Oil leak - Not a VCDS related question? or is it? • TFSI - Oil leak - TFSI - Oil leak - Not a VCDS related question? or is it?

Fault Code: P129F/004767 --- Possible Symptoms --- • MIL On • Reduced power --- Possible Causes --- • Faulty Fuel Pressure Sensor (G410) • Wiring Harness for Fuel Pressure Sensor (G410) --- Possible Solutions --- • Replace Fuel Pressure Sensor (G410) • Repair Damaged Wiring --- Special Notes --- • When found on Direct Injection engines, may also have DTC 012555 (P310B). Can be caused by a faulty G410 sensor. See TSB 01-07-69 (TB 2016182) for more information on testing and updated part numbers.

Fault Code: P130A/04874 --- Possible Causes --- • Excessive Misfiring --- Possible Solutions --- • Correct the root cause of the Excessive Misfiring --- Special Notes --- • This DTC indicates that the Engine Control Module (ECM) has seen 'Excessive Misfiring' and the the ECM has switched 'OFF' fuel injector(s) to prevent engine and exhaust catalyst damage • To clear this fault code, it will be necessary to correct the root cause of the misfires and this code will then be able to be cleared.

Fault Code: P13D3 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) ON --- Possible Causes --- • Engine controller software out of date.See special notes • Faulty Pressure Sensor/Glow Plugs • Carbon deposits on Pressure Sensor/Glow Plugs • If stored in conjunction with cylinder specific "Contribution/Balance" DTCs, low compression or engine mechanical issues may be responsibleFor example, mechanical problems including replacement Fuel Injectors that were not adapted to the engine controller using the Injector Quantity Adjustment (IMA) channels may cause this DTC. • For example, mechanical problems including replacement Fuel Injectors that were not adapted to the engine controller using the Injector Quantity Adjustment (IMA) channels may cause this DTC. • For example, mechanical problems including replacement Fuel Injectors that were not adapted to the engine controller using the Injector Quantity Adjustment (IMA) channels may cause this DTC. --- Possible Solutions --- • Check/Update Engine controller software.See special notes • Check/Replace Pressure Sensor/Glow Plugs • Check the Engine mechanical condition in addition to installed fuel injector(s) when stored in conjunction with cylinder specific "Contribution/Balance" DTCs. --- Special Notes --- • CR TDI engine controllers had several known software issues and dealer level flash updates over the years. Check theOfficial Factory Repair Informationif your engine controller is not listed below:Audi:When found in the NAR market Audi Q7 with CATA 3.0L CR TDI engine see TPI 2025155/1 • Audi:When found in the NAR market Audi Q7 with CATA 3.0L CR TDI engine see TPI 2025155/1 • When found in the NAR market Audi Q7 with CATA 3.0L CR TDI engine see TPI 2025155/1 • Audi:When found in the NAR market Audi Q7 with CATA 3.0L CR TDI engine see TPI 2025155/1 • When found in the NAR market Audi Q7 with CATA 3.0L CR TDI engine see TPI 2025155/1 • When found in the NAR market Audi Q7 with CATA 3.0L CR TDI engine see TPI 2025155/1

Fault Code: P150A/005386 --- Possible Causes --- • Fault Code stored in Instrument Cluster • If the battery was disconnected, the dash panel reset, resulting in the inability to transmit a time signal. --- Possible Solutions --- • Check Instrument Cluster for Fault Codes • Clear fault codes, start engine (idle for 30 seconds), switch ignition off, switch ignition on, check and clear codes if needed --- Special Notes --- • When found in VW Tiguan (5N): 2.0TFSI (CCTA), see TSB 01-08-20/TPI 2018467 for Details.Update ECU Software • Update ECU Software • Update ECU Software

Fault Code: P164B/005707 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Oil pressure warning active. --- Possible Causes --- • Faulty Oil Pressure Switch (F22). • Oil pressure low. --- Possible Solutions --- • Replace outdated Oil Pressure Switch. • Verify Oil pressure is within the manufacture's specifications, see theOfficial Factory Repair Information. --- Special Notes --- • When found in 2011 ~ 2014 Audi S4, S5, S5, Cabriolet, A6, A7, Q5, Q7 with 3.0L FSI vehicles, verify the Oil Pressure Switch (F22) is the updated type. • See TSB 01 14 85 2036761https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2021/MC-10204727-0001.pdfNHTSA • https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2021/MC-10204727-0001.pdfNHTSA • https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2021/MC-10204727-0001.pdfNHTSA

Fault Code: P16DB/005851 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Engine runs rough • Hesitation or lack of power • Increased fuel consumption • Illuminated MIL (Malfunction Indicator Light) --- Possible Causes --- • Spark plugs fouled or worn • Intake system leaks (false air) • Fuel pressure or fuel delivery issues • Faulty fuel injector(s) • Faulty ignition coil(s) --- Possible Solutions --- • Visually inspect and replace spark plugs if necessary • Inspect intake system for leaks and repair as needed • Check and verify correct fuel pressure and delivery quantity • Inspect and clean or replace fuel injectors • Check ignition coils, swap coils location to see if the fault code changes --- Special Notes --- • As per the Factory Repair manuals:This fault code sets when a specific cylinder runs too lean, causing an imbalance in the air-fuel mixture and engine roughness." • This fault code sets when a specific cylinder runs too lean, causing an imbalance in the air-fuel mixture and engine roughness." • Fault may be more prevalent in conditions such as:Engine speed between 1,240 – 2,240 RPMEngine load between 39.80 – 99.80%Cylinder head ECT > 60°CModeled catalyst temperature between 450 – 850°CLambda control in closed-loop operationNo rough road detectedIntegrated air mass within 1.70 – 3.50 kg range • Engine speed between 1,240 – 2,240 RPM • Engine load between 39.80 – 99.80% • Cylinder head ECT > 60°C • Modeled catalyst temperature between 450 – 850°C • Lambda control in closed-loop operation • No rough road detected • Integrated air mass within 1.70 – 3.50 kg range • This fault code sets when a specific cylinder runs too lean, causing an imbalance in the air-fuel mixture and engine roughness." • Engine speed between 1,240 – 2,240 RPM • Engine load between 39.80 – 99.80% • Cylinder head ECT > 60°C • Modeled catalyst temperature between 450 – 850°C • Lambda control in closed-loop operation • No rough road detected • Integrated air mass within 1.70 – 3.50 kg range

Fault Code: P1757 Standard OBD-II Code: P1757 --- Possible Symptoms --- • MIL on • Vehicle in limp mode --- Possible Causes --- • Fuse(s) faulty • Wiring and/or Connector(s) for Control Module faulty • Faulty Control Module --- Possible Solutions --- • Check fuse(s) • Check/Repair Wiring/Connector(s) • Replace faulty Control Module

Fault Code: P1888 Standard OBD-II Code: P1888 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Reduced heat output • Stalling, no-start, no communication to control module(s) --- Possible Causes --- • Coolant shut-off valve (N82) faulty • Wiring or power supply to/from N82 faulty --- Possible Solutions --- • Check Coolant shut-off valve (N82) • Check for applicable recalls or TPIs related to N82 failure resulting in coolant migration thorough the N82 solenoid connector resting in destroyed wiring harness(es) --- Special Notes --- Audi • If coolant migration damaged the wiring harnesses you may find this DTC in conjunction withP2755orP2753. VW • VW RoW market TPI 2043381 pertains to similar DTCs:P1889andP19A2. • Although VW does not have a TPI or recall related to N82 failure for this DTC, the following photo is from a 2013 RoW market (5G / Mk7 / MQB) Golf with CHHB 2.0T which destroyed wiring harness and resulted in a no-start:

Fault Code: P189A - Clutch 1: Clearance too Small --- Possible Causes --- • Clutch K1 free play is too little - 0AM Transmissions --- Possible Solutions --- • Replace multiple clutch - 0AM Transmissions --- Special Notes --- • When found in - Audi or Volkswagen vehicles with the 0AM Transmissions, please see:TPI-2021821 - Transmission: judders when pulling away or at constant speed, no gear change • TPI-2021821 - Transmission: judders when pulling away or at constant speed, no gear change • TPI-2021821 - Transmission: judders when pulling away or at constant speed, no gear change

Fault Code: P189C/006300 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Unable to complete Basic Settings, fault will not clear from Transmission Control Module. --- Possible Causes --- • Faulty wiring. • Faulty connections. • Faulty Fuse(s). • Faulty Mechatronic Unit, (V401-Hydraulic pump motor) --- Possible Solutions --- • Check/repair/replace faulty wiring/connections. • Check/replace faulty fuse(s). • Replace faulty Mechatronic Unit. --- Special Notes --- • On the 7-Speed Dual Clutch Transmission (DSG/0AM) transmission check for battery power at Pin 9 of the 25-Pin connector. • Once an effective repair has been completed, clear faults from Transmission Control Module and set Basic Settings.

Fault Code: P1A58/006744 - NOx catalytic converter: Regeneration required --- Possible Solutions --- • Perform "Regeneration for NOx Storage Catalytic Converter while Driving"CJAA engine, use Adaptation Channel [018], follow VCDS instructions after Security Access • CJAA engine, use Adaptation Channel [018], follow VCDS instructions after Security Access • See Volkswagen Tech Tip (TT_01-19-07) for helpful regeneration tips. For the NAR 2009 - 2014 vehicles with the 2,0l CBEA, CJAA TDI engines. See the link below:https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2019/MC-10169515-0001.pdfref:NHTSA.gov • https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2019/MC-10169515-0001.pdfref:NHTSA.gov • CJAA engine, use Adaptation Channel [018], follow VCDS instructions after Security Access • https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2019/MC-10169515-0001.pdfref:NHTSA.gov --- Special Notes --- • When found in following (see below) North American Audi and Volkswagen diesel vehicles, vehicle maybe part of the "VW Emissions Modification" and maybe covered under an extended warranty program.Audi A3 - 2010 to 2013 (unconfirmed)Volkswagen Golf, Jetta, Beetle - 2009 to 2014Volkswagen Passat - 2015 (unconfirmed) • Audi A3 - 2010 to 2013 (unconfirmed) • Volkswagen Golf, Jetta, Beetle - 2009 to 2014 • Volkswagen Passat - 2015 (unconfirmed) • Usually found with the fault codeP2BA6/01174 - NOx Exceedance - SCR NOx Catalyst Performance • Audi A3 - 2010 to 2013 (unconfirmed) • Volkswagen Golf, Jetta, Beetle - 2009 to 2014 • Volkswagen Passat - 2015 (unconfirmed) • For US market vehicles, use this website to verify, by VIN, the status of the vehicle in question.https://www.vwdiesellookup.comhttps://audidiesellookup.com • https://www.vwdiesellookup.com • https://audidiesellookup.com • Related information:VW Gen 1 TDI Booklet - pdf • VW Gen 1 TDI Booklet - pdf • https://www.vwdiesellookup.com • https://audidiesellookup.com • VW Gen 1 TDI Booklet - pdf • For Canada market vehicles, please see this website for more information:https://www.vw.ca/en/owners-and-drivers/tdi.html • https://www.vw.ca/en/owners-and-drivers/tdi.html • https://www.vw.ca/en/owners-and-drivers/tdi.html Internal note: When the NOx Catalyst is replaced on a CBEA/CJAA 2.0L see ST?ID=113085

content: Fault Code: P1B01 --- Special Notes --- • When P1B01 (or 8526354) is found in Address 19-CAN Gateway on a MQB Golf 7 based vehicle make sure the one-way roller clutch in the alternator pulley is not slipping under load.

Fault Code: P2122/008482 Standard OBD-II Code: P2122 --- Possible Symptoms --- • MIL On --- Possible Causes --- • Faulty Accelerator Pedal Assembly • Wiring Harness Issue for Accelerator Pedal Assembly --- Possible Solutions --- • Inspect Throttle Pedal and Wiring Harness. See Factory Repair Manual. --- Special Notes --- The signal voltage for the Accelerator Pedal could be shared with other engine components, O2 sensors for example.

Fault Code: P242F/009263 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) ON --- Possible Causes --- • Diesel Particle Filter (DPF) restricted/clogged • Diesel Particle Filter (DPF) faulty --- Possible Solutions --- • Check Engine related Fault Codes / Problems • Check Diesel Particle Filter (DPF) Load • Perform Diesel Particle Filter (DPF) Service Regeneration --- Special Notes --- • When found inVW Crafter (2E): 2.5l CR-TDI Engine (BJJ, BJK, BJL, BJM):Depending on the Model Year and/or if the Diesel Particle Filter (DPF) has been replaced already, different Generations of the Filter may be installed. If in doubt, check the Filter Part Number. Generation 2 (DPF2) were 2E0-254-700-E and 2E0-254-700-F, while Generation 3 (DPF3) were 2E0-254-700-G/GX.Check Engine Control Module (ECM) Software Level and installed Diesel Particle Filter (DPF) Part Number/Generation. Information on the latest Software Versions can be found in TPI 2020740 (DPF2) and TPI 2020722 (DPF3).When Replacing a DPF2 with DPF3, the Engine Control Module (ECM) Software Versionmustbe updated as well. Operating a DPF2 with DPF3 Software or vice versa will damage the DPF. For Details see TPI 2015609.If the Vehicle is used mainly for Short Trip/Distance Driving (for Example as a Delivery Truck), consider applying the special Short Trip/Distance Software as described in TPI 2018140. • Depending on the Model Year and/or if the Diesel Particle Filter (DPF) has been replaced already, different Generations of the Filter may be installed. If in doubt, check the Filter Part Number. Generation 2 (DPF2) were 2E0-254-700-E and 2E0-254-700-F, while Generation 3 (DPF3) were 2E0-254-700-G/GX. • Check Engine Control Module (ECM) Software Level and installed Diesel Particle Filter (DPF) Part Number/Generation. Information on the latest Software Versions can be found in TPI 2020740 (DPF2) and TPI 2020722 (DPF3). • When Replacing a DPF2 with DPF3, the Engine Control Module (ECM) Software Versionmustbe updated as well. Operating a DPF2 with DPF3 Software or vice versa will damage the DPF. For Details see TPI 2015609. • If the Vehicle is used mainly for Short Trip/Distance Driving (for Example as a Delivery Truck), consider applying the special Short Trip/Distance Software as described in TPI 2018140. • Depending on the Model Year and/or if the Diesel Particle Filter (DPF) has been replaced already, different Generations of the Filter may be installed. If in doubt, check the Filter Part Number. Generation 2 (DPF2) were 2E0-254-700-E and 2E0-254-700-F, while Generation 3 (DPF3) were 2E0-254-700-G/GX. • Check Engine Control Module (ECM) Software Level and installed Diesel Particle Filter (DPF) Part Number/Generation. Information on the latest Software Versions can be found in TPI 2020740 (DPF2) and TPI 2020722 (DPF3). • When Replacing a DPF2 with DPF3, the Engine Control Module (ECM) Software Versionmustbe updated as well. Operating a DPF2 with DPF3 Software or vice versa will damage the DPF. For Details see TPI 2015609. • If the Vehicle is used mainly for Short Trip/Distance Driving (for Example as a Delivery Truck), consider applying the special Short Trip/Distance Software as described in TPI 2018140.

Fault Code: P246E --- Possible Symptoms --- • Power Loss/Limp mode • MIL on • Glow Plug Light on • DPF Light on --- Possible Causes --- • EGT sensor faulty • EGT wiring faulty --- Possible Solutions --- • Check EGT sensor and wiring. • Note, when found in RoW B8 chassis A4 (8K) models with Engine code CAGA which uses the UDS protocol:The Advanced Measuring Values for EGT sensors may not be labeled correctly! Make sure to check [*ENG106430*]Exhaust gas temperature before particulate filtereven though this is the last (4th) sensor.Unplugging the suspect EGT sensor while re-checking the resulting fault codes and evaluating Advanced Measuring Values would be advised before replacing parts. • The Advanced Measuring Values for EGT sensors may not be labeled correctly! Make sure to check [*ENG106430*]Exhaust gas temperature before particulate filtereven though this is the last (4th) sensor. • Unplugging the suspect EGT sensor while re-checking the resulting fault codes and evaluating Advanced Measuring Values would be advised before replacing parts. • The Advanced Measuring Values for EGT sensors may not be labeled correctly! Make sure to check [*ENG106430*]Exhaust gas temperature before particulate filtereven though this is the last (4th) sensor. • Unplugging the suspect EGT sensor while re-checking the resulting fault codes and evaluating Advanced Measuring Values would be advised before replacing parts.

Fault Code: P24D8 --- Possible Symptoms --- • MIL on --- Possible Causes --- • EVAP Pressure Sensor (G950) faulty • EVAP Pressure Sensor (G950) --- Possible Solutions --- • Check EVAP Pressure Sensor (G950) sensor and wiring. • Note, this fault text corresponds to what is shown in the factory scan tool but may be partially incorrect (we think it should be " Pressure Sensor" instead of "Pressure Sensor 2". For example, in a 2019 Audi A6 3.0T with the DLZA engine, there is only one EVAP pressure sensor, not two. Unplugging the sensor G950 will set this fault. Some V8 applications may have multiple sensors and it's unclear if this fault is used with those or something different exists.

Fault Code: P2533 Standard OBD-II Code: P2533 --- Special Notes --- When found in the 4F chassis Audi A6: • Seehttps://forums.ross-tech.com/showthread.php?6433-Case-Study-2007-A6-crank-no-start-P2533 • Check the terminal 50 (Starter solenoid signal) wire from terminal 8/87 of the J53 relay -to- T17a/16 for damage in the E-Box. If that wire is OK make sure the starter cranks when bypassing the J53 relay before tracing the Black/Red wire to the Engine controller. When found in any other Audi using a similar 2-relay (J53 and J695) Starter system verify the wiring diagram matches the above notes before removing the wipers and opening the E-box.

Fault Code: P2556 Standard OBD-II Code: P2556 --- Possible Symptoms --- • MIL On • Cooling system malfunctioning --- Possible Causes --- • Faulty Engine Coolant Level Sensor (G32) • Faulty Engine Temperature Control Actuator (N493) • Wiring or power/ground issues to those components --- Possible Solutions --- • Check Engine Coolant Level Sensor (G32), Engine Temperature Control Actuator (N493) in addition to the related wiring per therepair manual. --- Special Notes --- • At face value you would initially believe this DTC is for the Engine Coolant Level Sensor however in most cases that sensor is wired to the 17-Instrument Cluster or the 09-Central Electronics. The Coolant Level Sensor is not usually wired directly to the 01-Engine control module. In those cases the thermostat (N493) and the wiring to it should be checked as per post #8 in this thread:https://forums.ross-tech.com/index.php?threads/28967/The example vehicle would use this generation 09-Cent. Elect. moduleMLB based Cent. Elect. (J519) BCM1and measuring block group 028.1 can be used to check the Coolant Level (G32) status. • The example vehicle would use this generation 09-Cent. Fault Code: P2755 Standard OBD-II Code: P2755 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Reduced heat output • Stalling, no-start, no communication to control module(s) --- Possible Causes --- • N82, N509, or N488 faulty • Wiring or power supply to/from N82, N509, or N488 faulty --- Possible Solutions --- • Check N82, N509, or N488 • Check for applicable recalls or TPIs related to N82 failure resulting in coolant migration thorough the N82 solenoid connector resting in destroyed wiring harness(es) --- Special Notes --- • This page contains a photo from a CHHB 2.0T Mk7 Golf after N82 and harness failure:P1888 • The following pages contains a photos from a CDRA (4H) 4.2L A8L after N82 damaged the entire wiring harness:2 pin connector14 pin connectorThat vehicle hadP2755stored in the TCM in conjunction with intermittent rough running and stalling concerns and various system too Rich fuel trim engine DTCs. • 2 pin connector • 14 pin connector • That vehicle hadP2755stored in the TCM in conjunction with intermittent rough running and stalling concerns and various system too Rich fuel trim engine DTCs. • When found in any number of 2011-2013 model year vehicles make sure to check for open recalls and TPIs for noted issues. These are just a few documents that were published:NAR (North American Region)2011-2013 model year Audi A8Recall Service Action Code: 80B8https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2016/MC-10128034-9999.pdfNHTSAFor 2011-2024 Audi vehicles, TPI 2033806 suggests checking/replacing N82, N509, or N488 as needed in addition to any damaged harnesses. Fault Code: P2BA6/01174 - NOx Exceedance - SCR NOx Catalyst Performance --- Special Notes --- • When found in following (see below) North American Audi and Volkswagen diesel vehicles, vehicle maybe part of the "VW Emissions Modification" and maybe covered under an extended warranty program.Audi A3 - 2010 to 2013 (unconfirmed)Volkswagen Golf, Jetta, Beetle - 2009 to 2014Volkswagen Passat - 2015 (unconfirmed) • Audi A3 - 2010 to 2013 (unconfirmed) • Volkswagen Golf, Jetta, Beetle - 2009 to 2014 • Volkswagen Passat - 2015 (unconfirmed) • Audi A3 - 2010 to 2013 (unconfirmed) • Volkswagen Golf, Jetta, Beetle - 2009 to 2014 • Volkswagen Passat - 2015 (unconfirmed) • Usually found with the fault codeP1A58/006744 - NOx catalytic converter: Regeneration required • For US market vehicles, use this website to verify, by VIN, the status of the vehicle in question.https://www.vwdiesellookup.comhttps://audidiesellookup.com • https://www.vwdiesellookup.com • https://audidiesellookup.com • Related information:VW Gen 1 TDI Booklet - pdf • VW Gen 1 TDI Booklet - pdf • https://www.vwdiesellookup.com • https://audidiesellookup.com • VW Gen 1 TDI Booklet - pdf • For Canada market vehicles, please see this website for more information:https://www.vw.ca/en/owners-and-drivers/tdi.html • https://www.vw.ca/en/owners-and-drivers/tdi.html • https://www.vw.ca/en/owners-and-drivers/tdi.html Internal note: When the NOx Catalyst is replaced on a CBEA/CJAA 2.0L see ST?ID=113085

content: Fault Code: P3053 Standard OBD-II Code: P3053 --- Special Notes --- • When found in MQB Golf based models such as the 2018 - 2019 (CDVC) 3.6L Vr6 VW Atlas verify the fuse to T91a/88 terminal of the ECM (623) labeled "SIG" in the wiring diagram is OK. A blown or missing fused power supply to that terminal of the ECM will result in this DTC. This was fuse SB22 on the 2019 Atlas we received feedback from.Wiring diagrams can be obtained from erWin directly:Official Factory Repair Information. • Wiring diagrams can be obtained from erWin directly:Official Factory Repair Information. • Wiring diagrams can be obtained from erWin directly:Official Factory Repair Information.

Fault Code: P307A/012410 --- Possible Symptoms --- • MIL on • Erratic idle • Poor engine performance under load --- Possible Causes --- • Dirty Throttle Body • Defective Throttle Body --- Possible Solutions --- • Clean Throttle Body and performThrottle Body Alignment (TBA) • Replace Throttle Body and performThrottle Body Alignment (TBA) --- Special Notes --- • When found with DTC16452/P0068/000104, clean the Throttle Body and performThrottle Body Alignment (TBA).

Fault Code: P310B/012555 --- Possible Symptoms --- • MIL On • Reduced power --- Possible Causes --- • Faulty Fuel Pressure Sensor (G410) • Wiring Harness for Fuel Pressure Sensor (G410) • Low Fuel PressureWeak Lift PumpRestriction in Fuel Filter or Fuel Lines • Weak Lift Pump • Restriction in Fuel Filter or Fuel Lines • Mechanical issue with High Pressure Fuel Pump • Replacement fuel filter not correct Bar rating (ex. 4.0 Bar vs 6.0 Bar) • Weak Lift Pump • Restriction in Fuel Filter or Fuel Lines --- Possible Solutions --- • Replace Fuel Pressure Sensor (G410) • Repair Damaged Wiring • Remove and inspect High Pressure Fuel Pump • Check Pressure Limiting Valve installed on the high pressure fuel rail when applicable --- Special Notes --- • When found inVW"FSI" Direct Injection engines, may also have DTCP129F/004767. Can be caused by a faulty G410 sensor. See TSB 01-07-69 or 01-11-11 (TPI 2016182) for more information on testing and updated part numbers. • When found inVW"FSI" Direct Injection engines, may also have DTCs P129F, P008B, P129E, P008A, P2540 and/or P0087. See TSB 01-11-28 (TPI 2027458) for information which is similar to the first noted TSB.https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2012/SB-10044939-4844.pdfNHTSA • https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2012/SB-10044939-4844.pdfNHTSA • When found inVW"FSI" Direct Injection engines (3.6L CDVC), may also have DTC P008B. See TT 20-18-06TT (TPI 2053320) for information on loose pins at G410 or G410 replacement.https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2018/MC-10154081-9999.pdfNHTSA • https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2018/MC-10154081-9999.pdfNHTSA • The G410 Fuel Pressure Sensor has a VW Warranty Extension in the US market due to premature failure. Regardless of the vehicle brand, contact the local dealer with the full VIN to see if warranty extensions, recalls or RVU (required vehicle updates) apply. • When found inAudi2.0T "FSI" Direct Injection engines, may also have DTCsP129F/004767, P2293 and/or P0087. See TSB 24-08-58 (TPI 2017356/2) for more information on testing and updated part numbers. • When found on Direct Injection engines, such as the 2.0T, in combination with system too lean or similar faults indicating a vacuum leak address the cause of those first. Common fault codes are foundhere • When this fault is stored after all of the typical failure items have been replaced or tested, verify the electric fuel pump and/or FPCM (Fuel Pump Control Module) is operating properly. Low fuel pressure or volume may be the end result of a fuel supply problem including an incorrect fuel filter when the pressure regulator is integral.When found on FSI engines, MVB 106.2 shows the duty cycle of the lift pump, this value should be below 70%. The higher the number, the more the lift pump has to run to maintain fuel pressure to the High Pressure Fuel Pump.This video may be helpful: • When found on FSI engines, MVB 106.2 shows the duty cycle of the lift pump, this value should be below 70%. The higher the number, the more the lift pump has to run to maintain fuel pressure to the High Pressure Fuel Pump. • This video may be helpful: • https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2012/SB-10044939-4844.pdfNHTSA • https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2018/MC-10154081-9999.pdfNHTSA • When found on FSI engines, MVB 106.2 shows the duty cycle of the lift pump, this value should be below 70%. The higher the number, the more the lift pump has to run to maintain fuel pressure to the High Pressure Fuel Pump. • This video may be helpful: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jThQVzYFbVo • We have had several reports where the intank Lift Pump was replaced with an "OE" unit from a 3rd party supplier and still have issues. Our suggestion is to purchase a proper OE unit from the dealer's parts department.

Fault Code: P310C/012556 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Lamp (MIL) active --- Possible Causes --- • Fuel Reservoir empty • Fuel Filter clocked • Fuel Pump (G6) faulty --- Possible Solutions --- • Check Fuel Level • Check Fuel Filter • Check Fuel Pump (G6)

Fault Code: P320C/012812 --- Possible Causes --- • Exhaust Leak before O2 Sensor • False air leak in the intake system after the MAF sensor • Failed O2 Sensors --- Possible Solutions --- • Repair exhaust Leak before O2 Sensor • Repair false air leak in the intake system after the MAF sensor • Replace failed O2 Sensors • Reset O2 Sensor adapted values, see notes below --- Special Notes --- • When found in NAR 2009 - 2010 Volkswagen Jetta with a 2.0L TDI CBEA or CJAA, a new ECM software version is available, please see:TSB 23-11-01TPI 2025546Required Vehicle Update – 23H1 • TSB 23-11-01 • TPI 2025546 • Required Vehicle Update – 23H1 • TSB 23-11-01 • TPI 2025546 • Required Vehicle Update – 23H1 • Check Measuring Value Blocks (CBEA/CJAA) Group 041-(B1S1) and 136-(B1S2) for O2 Sensor adapted valuesNormal Range is [-1.28 to 1.22] - if out of range suspect failed O2 SensorsTo reset the above value after a repair, disconnect the O2 sensors, cycle the ignition on/off for 5 seconds and reconnect O2 sensors, clear codes and check MVB data again. • Normal Range is [-1.28 to 1.22] - if out of range suspect failed O2 SensorsTo reset the above value after a repair, disconnect the O2 sensors, cycle the ignition on/off for 5 seconds and reconnect O2 sensors, clear codes and check MVB data again. • To reset the above value after a repair, disconnect the O2 sensors, cycle the ignition on/off for 5 seconds and reconnect O2 sensors, clear codes and check MVB data again. • Normal Range is [-1.28 to 1.22] - if out of range suspect failed O2 SensorsTo reset the above value after a repair, disconnect the O2 sensors, cycle the ignition on/off for 5 seconds and reconnect O2 sensors, clear codes and check MVB data again. • To reset the above value after a repair, disconnect the O2 sensors, cycle the ignition on/off for 5 seconds and reconnect O2 sensors, clear codes and check MVB data again. • To reset the above value after a repair, disconnect the O2 sensors, cycle the ignition on/off for 5 seconds and reconnect O2 sensors, clear codes and check MVB data again.

Fault Code: P334B/013131 --- Possible Symptoms --- • Malfunction Indicator Light (MIL) and/or Exhaust Warning Lamp active • Reduced Idle quality and Engine Performance --- Possible Causes --- • Faulty Charge Pressure Control Actuator • Faulty Turbocharger • Wiring issues to/from Charge Pressure Control Actuator • Mechanical adjustment problem with Charge Pressure Control Actuator and Turbocharger.Engine Controller software issue with 1.2l TFSI engines.See Special Notes • Engine Controller software issue with 1.2l TFSI engines.See Special Notes • Engine Controller software issue with 1.2l TFSI engines.See Special Notes --- Possible Solutions --- • Check Charge Pressure Control Actuator and Turbocharger • Check wiring to/from Charge Pressure Control Actuator • Check the Mechanical adjustment of the Charge Pressure Control Actuator and TurbochargerCheck TPIs for known Engine Controller software issues with 1.2l TFSI engines.See Special Notes • Check TPIs for known Engine Controller software issues with 1.2l TFSI engines.See Special Notes • Check TPIs for known Engine Controller software issues with 1.2l TFSI engines.See Special Notes --- Special Notes --- • When found in 1.2l TFSI engines check theOfficial Factory Repair Informationfor TPIs that cover known issues with this DTC in addition toP334C. The following TPIs may be helpful:Audi TPI# 2027809/9VW Commercial (i.e. VW Caddy) TPI# 2031930/1VW TPI# 2027485/7 • Audi TPI# 2027809/9 • VW Commercial (i.e. VW Caddy) TPI# 2031930/1 • VW TPI# 2027485/7 • When found in the 1.4T engines after wastegate actuator or turbo replacement, this forum post may be helpful:https://forums.ross-tech.com/showthread.php?23664-Case-Study-1-4T-CZTA-P056E-and-P334B-after-new-turbo-install • https://forums.ross-tech.com/showthread.php?23664-Case-Study-1-4T-CZTA-P056E-and-P334B-after-new-turbo-install • Audi TPI# 2027809/9 • VW Commercial (i.e. VW Caddy) TPI# 2031930/1 • VW TPI# 2027485/7 • https://forums.ross-tech.com/showthread.php?23664-Case-Study-1-4T-CZTA-P056E-and-P334B-after-new-turbo-install

Fault Code: U102F/053295 --- Possible Causes --- • Wiring, connections concerns or faulty G611-Radiator Identification Sensor --- Possible Solutions --- • Correct wiring, connection concerns or replace failed G611-Radiator Identification Sensor (w/radiator). --- Special Notes --- • Currently, the G611-Radiator Identification Sensor is not available separately, it comes with a new radiator when ordered from the vehicle's manufacture. The sensor will need to be attached to the radiator with AMV-195-KD1-01 adhesive, See Volkswagen Tech Tip 19-12-01 for more information.https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2015/MC-10122979-9999.pdfNHTSA • https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2015/MC-10122979-9999.pdfNHTSA • https://static.nhtsa.gov/odi/tsbs/2015/MC-10122979-9999.pdfNHTSA • The cooling surface of the radiator on some Volkswagen/Audi Group vehicles is coated with a catalyst that helps reduce Ozone, The G611 sensor monitors this function and is part of the federal emissions control of these vehicles which allows the manufacture to receive EPA 'credits'.This technology is called PremAir®, see the BASF web site for more information.https://catalysts.basf.com/products-and-industries/mobile-emissions-catalysts/specialized-catalysts/premair • This technology is called PremAir®, see the BASF web site for more information. • https://catalysts.basf.com/products-and-industries/mobile-emissions-catalysts/specialized-catalysts/premair • This technology is called PremAir®, see the BASF web site for more information. • https://catalysts.basf.com/products-and-industries/mobile-emissions-catalysts/specialized-catalysts/premair • Note: The fault code 'U1030-LIN bus inactive' may also be present,this should clear when the U102F fault has been corrected.

Fault Code: U1052/053330 --- Possible Causes --- • Faulty wiring/connections • Faulty Generator/Alternator --- Possible Solutions --- • Correct wiring, connection concerns • Replace faulty Generator/Alternator --- Special Notes --- • When found on vehicles with 'Coupling Point-TML', (8-Pin connector), check for possible wiring damage. 'Coupling Point-TML' can be located near left frame rail, below left headlight. Wire damage found on a 2018 NAR Tiguan (Images Courtesy of: J&M Automotive) • LIN-Bus wire for Generator(purple/white) • TML - Coupling Point (T8)(8 - pin connector)

Fault Code: U1122 --- Special Notes --- • When this exact DTC is found in Address 3C: Lane Change (J769) on the F3 chassis Q3 the cause is most likely buggy factory software: • Check for updated TPIs.
